title: Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus
intro: Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus, also known as the Pincushion Cactus, is a species of cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its unique, spherical shape and sharp, needle-like spines. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. Bioactive constituents of Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often employed in traditional medicine for their therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pachycereeae and the genus Echinofossulocactus. The classification of Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus at the rank of species is unranked, as it is sometimes considered as a synonym of Echinofossulocactus viridiflorus. However, Echinofossulocactus phyllacanthus is also sometimes treated as a valid species. The classification of this species may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomic understanding.
